charts.cvddSubtitle: Cumulative Value Coin Days Destroyed, a historically useful bottom indicator.
charts.noData: CVDD chart data is not available yet.
charts.powerLawSubtitle: A long-term price model based on power-law regression since Bitcoin genesis.
charts.rainbowSubtitle: A logarithmic growth curve with color-coded valuation bands.
charts.rainbowLoadFailed: We could not load the chart data. Please try again.
charts.rainbow.about: The Bitcoin Rainbow Chart uses logarithmic growth curves to identify market cycle positions. Nine color-coded bands represent valuation levels from "Fire Sale" (deep undervaluation) to "Maximum Bubble Territory" (extreme overvaluation).
charts.rainbow.interpretation.waiting: Waiting for the latest rainbow band calculation.
charts.rainbow.interpretation.depressed: Bitcoin is in a historically depressed valuation zone. Cooler bands have often represented long-term accumulation opportunities.
charts.rainbow.interpretation.fairValue: Bitcoin is near fair-value to accumulation territory. This zone has historically represented constructive long-term positioning.
charts.rainbow.interpretation.warm: Bitcoin is in a warmer valuation zone. Historical cycles suggest risk management becomes more important as price moves higher through the bands.
charts.rainbow.interpretation.overheated: Bitcoin is in an overheated valuation zone. Upper bands have historically appeared near speculative market-cycle extremes.
charts.rainbow.dataSource.formula: Calculation: Rainbow bands calculated from logarithmic regression model fit to historical price data since 2009-01-03
chartDetail.placeholder: Interactive chart rendering will be added in the next chart visualization story.
charts.mvrvSubtitle: Market value compared with realized value, used to spot statistically extreme cycle positions.
charts.mvrvLoadFailed: We could not load the chart data. Please try again.
charts.mvrv.noData: MVRV Z-Score chart data is not available yet.
charts.exchangeReserveSubtitle: Aggregate BTC held on exchanges — the accumulated result of exchange in/outflows
charts.exchangeReserveLoadFailed: Chart data could not be loaded. Please try again.
charts.exchangeReserve.noData: Exchange reserve data is not available yet.
charts.exchangeReserve.about: Exchange Reserve tracks the total quantity of BTC held in wallets controlled by exchanges — the accumulated result of exchange in/outflows. A rising reserve signals more coins are available for sale (bearish); a falling reserve signals coins are leaving exchanges for private custody, implying accumulation and increasing scarcity (bullish).
charts.exchangeReserve.interpretation.ri: Exchange reserves have risen {$INTERPOLATION}% over the past 30 days. A rising reserve means more coins are moving onto exchanges and becoming available for sale, historically associated with increasing selling pressure and decreasing scarcity — a bearish signal.
charts.exchangeReserve.interpretation.fa: Exchange reserves have fallen {$INTERPOLATION}% over the past 30 days. A falling reserve means coins are being withdrawn from exchanges into private custody, historically associated with accumulation and increasing scarcity — a bullish signal.
charts.exchangeReserve.interpretation.st: Exchange reserves have been roughly stable over the past 30 days, suggesting no strong shift in aggregate selling or accumulation pressure.
charts.exchangeReserve.dataSource.price: Bitcoin price: CoinGecko API via backend price history
charts.exchangeReserve.dataSource.reserv: Exchange reserve: CoinMetrics Community API (SplyExNtv) — aggregate BTC supply held in known exchange wallets, full history since 2011
charts.library.exchange-reserve.title: Bitcoin: Exchange Reserve
charts.library.exchange-reserve.descript: Tracks the total BTC held on exchanges. Rising reserves imply more coins available for sale (bearish); falling reserves imply accumulation and increasing scarcity (bullish).
charts.fundingRateSubtitle: Perpetual futures leverage — funding rate and open interest as a gauge of euphoria and squeeze risk
charts.fundingRateLoadFailed: Chart data could not be loaded. Please try again.
charts.fundingRate.noData: Funding rate data is not available yet.
charts.fundingRate.about: Perpetual futures funding rate and open interest measure leverage in the derivatives market. Positive funding means longs pay shorts (bullish/leveraged demand); negative funding means shorts pay longs (bearish/leveraged demand). Extremes in either direction, especially combined with rising open interest, have historically marked overheated conditions prone to sharp reversals.
charts.fundingRate.interpretation.overhe: Funding is strongly positive, meaning long positions are paying shorts to stay open. This reflects euphoric, leveraged long demand — historically associated with overheated conditions and a higher risk of long-squeeze cascades.
charts.fundingRate.interpretation.negati: Funding is negative, meaning short positions are paying longs to stay open. This reflects a short-heavy, fearful market — historically these setups have preceded short-squeeze rallies.
charts.fundingRate.interpretation.neutra: Funding is close to neutral, indicating no strong leveraged bias in either direction.
charts.fundingRate.dataSource.price: Bitcoin price: CoinGecko API via backend price history
charts.fundingRate.dataSource.funding: Funding rate: Binance Futures public API (BTCUSDT perpetual), daily average of 8-hour funding events, full history since 2019
charts.fundingRate.dataSource.oi: Open interest: Bybit public API (BTCUSDT linear perpetual), converted to USD using each day's BTC price. Full history available since 2020-08-05.
charts.library.funding-rate-oi.title: Funding Rate & Open Interest
charts.library.funding-rate-oi.descripti: Tracks perpetual futures funding rate and open interest as a gauge of leveraged market sentiment. Extreme positive funding with rising open interest has historically marked overheated, squeeze-prone conditions.
landing.model.fundingRateOi.tooltip: Tracks perpetual futures funding rate and open interest as a gauge of leveraged market sentiment. Extreme positive funding with rising open interest has historically marked overheated, squeeze-prone conditions.
charts.exchangeNetflowSubtitle: Daily net BTC flow on/off exchanges — the leading edge of the Exchange Reserve trend
charts.exchangeNetflowLoadFailed: Chart data could not be loaded. Please try again.
charts.exchangeNetflow.noData: Exchange netflow data is not available yet.
charts.exchangeNetflow.about: Exchange Netflow measures the daily difference between coins deposited onto and withdrawn from known exchange wallets. It is the leading edge of the cumulative Exchange Reserve trend — sudden whale deposits or withdrawals show up here immediately, before they move the aggregate reserve level. Positive netflow (net inflow) signals coins becoming available for sale (bearish); negative netflow (net outflow) signals coins leaving exchanges for private custody (bullish).
charts.exchangeNetflow.interpretation.in: Exchange netflow has averaged {$INTERPOLATION} per day over the past 7 days. A positive netflow means more coins are moving onto exchanges than off, historically associated with increasing selling pressure and decreasing scarcity — a bearish signal.
charts.exchangeNetflow.interpretation.ou: Exchange netflow has averaged {$INTERPOLATION} per day over the past 7 days. A negative netflow means more coins are being withdrawn from exchanges than deposited, historically associated with accumulation and increasing scarcity — a bullish signal.
charts.exchangeNetflow.interpretation.ne: Exchange netflow has been roughly balanced over the past 7 days, suggesting no strong shift in aggregate selling or accumulation pressure.
charts.exchangeNetflow.dataSource.price: Bitcoin price: CoinGecko API via backend price history
charts.exchangeNetflow.dataSource.netflo: Exchange netflow: CoinMetrics Community API (FlowInExNtv minus FlowOutExNtv) — daily net BTC flow into known exchange wallets, full history since 2011
charts.library.exchange-netflow.title: Bitcoin: Exchange Netflow
charts.library.exchange-netflow.signal: Daily flow — leading edge of reserve trend
charts.library.exchange-netflow.descript: Tracks the daily net BTC flow into or out of exchanges. Positive netflow implies more coins available for sale (bearish); negative netflow implies accumulation and increasing scarcity (bullish).
landing.model.exchangeNetflow.tooltip: Tracks the daily net BTC flow into or out of exchanges — the leading edge of the Exchange Reserve trend. Positive netflow implies more coins available for sale (bearish); negative netflow implies accumulation and increasing scarcity (bullish).
charts.realizedCapSubtitle: Market cap against the aggregate on-chain cost basis, in dollar terms
charts.realizedCapLoadFailed: Chart data could not be loaded. Please try again.
charts.realizedCap.noData: Realized cap data is not available yet.
charts.realizedCap.about: Realized Cap values every coin at the price it last moved on-chain, then sums those values across circulating supply — an estimate of aggregate cost basis in dollar terms. Market Cap uses the current price instead. Comparing the two shows how far the market trades above or below the aggregate on-chain cost basis, the same relationship the MVRV Z-Score is built from, presented here as raw dollar values rather than a standardized score.
charts.realizedCap.interpretation.overhe: Market cap trades at {$INTERPOLATION}x realized cap. Aggregate holders are deeply in profit, a pattern historically seen in late-cycle conditions.
charts.realizedCap.interpretation.elevat: Market cap trades at {$INTERPOLATION}x realized cap. Most market participants are holding sizeable paper profits.
charts.realizedCap.interpretation.fair: Market cap trades at {$INTERPOLATION}x realized cap. The market is in aggregate profit but still close to its on-chain cost basis.
charts.realizedCap.interpretation.stress: Market cap trades at {$INTERPOLATION}x realized cap. Aggregate holders are underwater, a pattern historically clustered near major cycle lows.
charts.realizedCap.dataSource.price: Bitcoin price: CoinGecko API via backend price history
charts.realizedCap.dataSource.realized: Realized price: stored DB values plus CoinMetrics community API history derived from PriceUSD / CapMVRVCur
charts.realizedCap.dataSource.calculatio: Market cap and realized cap: price (or realized price) multiplied by circulating supply, computed by the backend
charts.library.realized-cap.title: Bitcoin Realized Cap
charts.library.realized-cap.signal: Market cap vs aggregate cost basis
charts.library.realized-cap.description: Compares Bitcoin market cap with realized cap in dollar terms — the same relationship the MVRV Z-Score is built from, shown as raw cap values rather than a standardized score.
landing.model.realizedCap.tooltip: Compares market cap with realized cap in dollar terms, the same relationship behind the MVRV Z-Score presented as raw cap values.
charts.lthSthSoprSplitSubtitle: Long-term and short-term holder profit-taking, shown separately instead of as a combined ratio
charts.lthSthSoprSplitLoadFailed: Chart data could not be loaded. Please try again.
charts.lthSthSoprSplit.noData: LTH/STH SOPR data is not available yet.
charts.lthSthSoprSplit.about: LTH-SOPR and STH-SOPR measure the realized profit or loss ratio (sale price divided by acquisition price) for coins moved on-chain by long-term holders (held 155+ days) and short-term holders separately. A value above 1 means that cohort is on aggregate selling at a profit; below 1 means it is on aggregate selling at a loss. Viewing the two cohorts separately is sharper than the combined SOPR Ratio, since LTH and STH capitulation carry very different implications for cycle stage.
charts.lthSthSoprSplit.interpretation.lt: LTH SOPR is {$INTERPOLATION}, below 1 — long-term holders are on aggregate selling at a loss. This behavior has historically clustered near major cycle bottoms, since it takes a deep, prolonged drawdown to force experienced holders to capitulate.
charts.lthSthSoprSplit.interpretation.st: STH SOPR is {$INTERPOLATION}, below 1 — short-term holders are on aggregate selling at a loss, a common short-term pullback or capitulation signature that recurs more frequently than LTH capitulation.
charts.lthSthSoprSplit.interpretation.pr: Both LTH and STH SOPR are above 1 — both cohorts are on aggregate realizing profit, typical of stable-to-bullish market conditions.
charts.lthSthSoprSplit.dataSource.price: Bitcoin price: CoinGecko API via backend price history
charts.lthSthSoprSplit.dataSource.lth: LTH SOPR: bitcoin-data.com free public API, ingested daily and stored server-side (available from 25 June 2022)
charts.lthSthSoprSplit.dataSource.sth: STH SOPR: bitcoin-data.com free public API, ingested daily and stored server-side (available from 25 June 2022)
charts.library.lth-sth-sopr-split.title: LTH-SOPR / STH-SOPR Split
charts.library.lth-sth-sopr-split.descri: Shows long-term and short-term holder SOPR separately instead of as a combined ratio. LTH capitulation below 1 has historically clustered near major cycle bottoms.
landing.model.lthSthSoprSplit.tooltip: Splits SOPR into long-term and short-term holder cohorts. LTH capitulation below 1 has historically clustered near major cycle bottoms.
charts.googleTrendsBitcoinSubtitle: Worldwide "bitcoin" search interest, a classic retail-euphoria proxy
charts.googleTrendsBitcoinLoadFailed: Chart data could not be loaded. Please try again.
charts.googleTrendsBitcoin.noData: Google Trends search interest data is not available yet.
charts.googleTrendsBitcoin.about: Tracks worldwide Google search interest for "bitcoin" over time, scaled 0-100 relative to the peak search volume in the selected date range. It is a classic retail-attention and euphoria proxy that complements the Fear & Greed Index — search interest tends to spike hardest when new, less-experienced participants are entering the market.
charts.googleTrendsBitcoin.interpretatio: Waiting for Google Trends search interest data.
Worldwide "bitcoin" search interest is {$INTERPOLATION}, near its all-time relative peak. Historically, spikes this high have coincided with retail-driven euphoria late in a bull cycle.
Worldwide "bitcoin" search interest is {$INTERPOLATION}, moderately elevated. Retail attention is picking up but not yet at euphoric extremes.
Worldwide "bitcoin" search interest is {$INTERPOLATION}, relatively quiet. Low retail search attention has historically been more common during accumulation phases than at cycle tops.
charts.googleTrendsBitcoin.dataSource.pr: Bitcoin price: CoinGecko API via backend price history
charts.googleTrendsBitcoin.dataSource.se: Search interest: Google Trends' unofficial public endpoint for the worldwide "bitcoin" search term. This endpoint is undocumented and can change or rate-limit without notice, so data may occasionally be delayed or unavailable.
charts.library.google-trends-bitcoin.des: Tracks worldwide Google search interest for "bitcoin", a classic retail-euphoria proxy that complements the Fear & Greed Index.
landing.model.googleTrendsBitcoin.toolti: Tracks worldwide Google search interest for bitcoin, a classic retail-euphoria proxy that complements the Fear & Greed Index.
charts.soprRatioSubtitle: Long-term holder SOPR divided by short-term holder SOPR.
charts.soprRatioLoadFailed: We could not load the chart data. Please try again.
charts.soprRatioNoData: SOPR Ratio data is not available yet.
charts.piCycleSubtitle: A moving-average crossover signal for potential Bitcoin cycle tops.
charts.piCycleLoadFailed: We could not load the chart data. Please try again.
charts.puellSubtitle: Daily miner revenue compared with its 365-day average, useful for spotting miner stress and euphoria.
charts.puellLoadFailed: We could not load the chart data. Please try again.
charts.stockToFlowSubtitle: A scarcity-driven model price compared with Bitcoin market price.
charts.stockToFlowLoadFailed: We could not load the chart data. Please try again.
charts.library.stock-to-flow.title: Stock-to-Flow Model
charts.library.stock-to-flow.description: Tracks Bitcoin scarcity using circulating supply and issuance flow, with a simplified model price for long-term valuation context.
charts.library.bitcoin-rainbow.title: Bitcoin Rainbow Price Chart
charts.library.bitcoin-rainbow.descripti: Compares price against logarithmic cycle bands so cooler accumulation zones and overheated market periods are easy to scan.
charts.library.pi-cycle-top.title: Pi Cycle Top Indicator
charts.library.pi-cycle-top.description: Compares the 111-day moving average with twice the 350-day moving average to identify potential cycle-top signals.
charts.library.mvrv-z-score.title: MVRV Z-Score
charts.library.mvrv-z-score.description: Compares Bitcoin market cap to realized cap using a Z-Score to identify statistically extreme overvaluation and undervaluation periods.
charts.library.sopr-ratio.title: SOPR Ratio (LTH/STH)
charts.library.sopr-ratio.description: Divides long-term holder SOPR by short-term holder SOPR to compare profit-taking behavior between experienced holders and newer market participants.
charts.library.puell-multiple.title: Puell Multiple
charts.library.puell-multiple.descriptio: Measures daily miner revenue against its 365-day moving average to identify miner stress buy zones and high-profit sell zones.
charts.library.bitcoin-power-law.title: Bitcoin Power Law Chart
charts.library.bitcoin-power-law.descrip: Models Bitcoin price as a power function of time since genesis, with floor and ceiling bands that have historically contained price action across market cycles.
charts.library.bitcoin-cvdd.title: Bitcoin CVDD
charts.library.bitcoin-cvdd.description: Cumulative Value Coin Days Destroyed tracks the accumulated value-time of coin movements relative to market age. It has historically marked major Bitcoin price bottoms.
charts.library.halving-spiral.title: Bitcoin Halving Spiral
charts.library.halving-spiral.descriptio: Plots Bitcoin price on a polar chart where one full rotation equals one halving cycle. The logarithmic radius overlays cycles so bull and bear phases can be compared.
charts.library.vdd-multiple.title: VDD Multiple
charts.library.vdd-multiple.description: Compares the 30-day moving average of Value Days Destroyed (CDD × price) with its 365-day average. Peaks above 2.9 can signal cycle tops as long-term holders sell heavily; values below 0.75 suggest bear-market accumulation phases.
charts.library.halving-progress.title: Bitcoin Halving Progress
charts.library.halving-progress.descript: Shows Bitcoin price history across all halving cycles on a logarithmic scale. Cycle backgrounds, halving markers, and progress indicators show where the current cycle sits versus prior cycles.
charts.library.compare-bull-markets.desc: Aligns Bitcoin bull-market breakouts by days since price cleared the prior all-time high, scaling earlier eras to the current reward era for like-for-like cycle comparison.
charts.library.2yr-ma-multiplier.title: 2-Year MA Multiplier
charts.library.2yr-ma-multiplier.descrip: A Bitcoin investor tool: buy below the 2-year moving average and sell above the 2-year MA ×5. Intermediate multiplier bands show the degree of market overheating.
charts.library.price-forecast-tools.desc: A price forecast chart using BTC price, Top Cap, Delta Top, and CVDD to identify historically reliable Bitcoin cycle-top and bear-market-floor targets.
charts.library.mayer-multiple.title: Mayer Multiple
charts.library.mayer-multiple.descriptio: Bitcoin price divided by its 200-day moving average. Values above 2.4 have historically signaled overheated markets, while values below 1.0 have signaled undervaluation and long-term accumulation.
charts.library.200-week-ma-heatmap.title: 200-Week MA Heatmap
charts.library.200-week-ma-heatmap.signa: Long-term cycle floor and upper range
charts.library.200-week-ma-heatmap.descr: Colors price by its ratio to the 200-week moving average. The 200-week MA has historically acted as a final bear-market support, while colors above it show bull-market extension.
charts.library.fear-greed-index.title: Fear & Greed Index
charts.library.fear-greed-index.descript: Composite sentiment score from volatility, volume, social media, and surveys. Extreme fear has historically been a reliable long-term buy signal; extreme greed suggests caution.
charts.library.hash-ribbons.title: Hash Ribbons
charts.library.hash-ribbons.description: Compares the 30-day and 60-day moving averages of Bitcoin hash rate. A recovery crossover after miner capitulation has historically produced strong long-term buy signals.
charts.library.difficulty-ribbon.title: Difficulty Ribbon
charts.library.difficulty-ribbon.descrip: Layers multiple moving averages of mining difficulty. When short-term averages fall below longer-term averages, the ribbon compresses, signaling miner capitulation and historically cheap BTC.
charts.library.nvt-ratio.title: NVT Ratio
charts.library.nvt-ratio.description: Network Value to Transactions ratio: Bitcoin market cap divided by daily on-chain transaction volume. High NVT can signal overvaluation relative to usage; low NVT can suggest undervaluation.
charts.library.thermocap-multiple.title: Thermocap Multiple
charts.library.thermocap-multiple.signal: Market cap vs cumulative miner spend
charts.library.thermocap-multiple.descri: Market cap divided by cumulative total miner revenue. It measures how expensive Bitcoin is relative to all security spend ever made; historically high multiples often coincided with cycle tops.
charts.library.excess-liquidity.title: Excess Liquidity Lead Indicator
charts.library.excess-liquidity.descript: Compares the 1-year change in the US 3-month/10-year yield spread with a 6-month-forward excess liquidity indicator. Positive and rising liquidity has historically preceded easier financial conditions and stronger risk appetite.
charts.library.spx-liquidity.title: S&P 500 vs Excess Liquidity
charts.library.spx-liquidity.description: Compares the S&P 500 year-over-year change with the excess liquidity lead indicator. Positive turns in excess liquidity have historically led equity market advances by roughly 6 months.

trading.title: Trading plans and market signals
trading.subtitle: A combined view of chart signals, model-based price targets, and your personal trade plans.
trading.signals.configureAriaLabel: Configure signals used in your score
trading.signals.previewPlaceholder: Hover or focus a signal below to see its explanation here.
trading.projections.disclaimerText: These projections are based on on-chain and mathematical models (Stock-to-Flow, Mayer Multiple, Rainbow bands, Terminal Price, CVDD). They reflect historical model behaviour and are not financial advice. Always conduct your own research.
trading.create.title: Create Trading Plan
trading.form.title: Plan title
trading.form.notesPlaceholder: Why this trade, what signals support it...
trading.close.title: Close position
trading.close.description: Enter the price where you closed this position.
trading.signals.subtext.strongAccumulati: Multiple indicators align for a long-term buy opportunity.
trading.signals.subtext.favourable: Most indicators are bullish. Gradual accumulation may be justified.
trading.signals.subtext.mixed: Signals are conflicting. High-conviction positions carry more risk.
trading.signals.subtext.elevatedRisk: Several indicators show overvaluation. Reducing exposure may be prudent.
trading.signals.subtext.extremeCaution: Most indicators signal overvaluation or cycle-top risk. Avoid new longs.
trading.interpretation.extremeFear: Extreme fear - historically strong buy signal
trading.interpretation.nupl.capitulation: Capitulation - market in aggregate loss, historically strong accumulation zone
trading.interpretation.nupl.hope: Hope / Fear - near aggregate cost basis, early recovery conditions
trading.interpretation.nupl.optimism: Optimism / Anxiety - profitable but not euphoric
trading.interpretation.nupl.belief: Belief / Denial - mature bull-market profit zone
trading.interpretation.nupl.euphoria: Euphoria / Greed - overheated profit zone, cycle-top risk
trading.interpretation.belowMinerRevenue: Below average miner revenue - accumulation zone
trading.interpretation.mayerWellBelow: Well below 200-day MA - deep accumulation zone
trading.interpretation.piMovingToCrossov: Moving toward crossover - watch for top
trading.interpretation.piCloseCrossover: Very close to crossover - high risk
trading.interpretation.piCrossoverOccurr: Crossover occurred - historical cycle top signal
trading.interpretation.puellMinerStresse: Miners stressed - historically rare buy zone
trading.interpretation.puellElevatedMine: Elevated miner revenue - late cycle signal
trading.interpretation.s2fDeepDiscount: Deep discount to Stock-to-Flow model price
trading.interpretation.s2fMeaningfulDisc: Meaningful discount to Stock-to-Flow model price
trading.interpretation.s2fLargePremium: Large premium to Stock-to-Flow model price
trading.interpretation.projBelowFloor: Below modeled floor range - historically stressed valuation
trading.interpretation.projMidRange: Mid-range between modeled floor and terminal price
trading.interpretation.projNearTerminal: Near or above terminal price range
trading.interpretation.volDeeplyBelow: Deeply below 365-day trend band - capitulation-style discount
trading.interpretation.volFarAbove: Far above 365-day trend band - overheated volatility extension
trading.interpretation.m2StrongExpansion: Strong liquidity expansion - supportive macro backdrop for Bitcoin
trading.interpretation.m2PositiveGrowth: Positive liquidity growth - risk-on conditions improving
trading.interpretation.m2MildExpansion: Mild liquidity expansion - modest macro support
trading.interpretation.m2SevereContracti: Severe liquidity contraction - strong macro headwind
trading.interpretation.dxyWeakeningSharp: Dollar weakening sharply - strong macro tailwind for Bitcoin
trading.interpretation.dxyStable: Dollar broadly stable - neutral macro impulse
trading.interpretation.dxyStrengthening: Dollar strengthening - macro headwind for Bitcoin
trading.interpretation.dxyStrengtheningS: Dollar strengthening sharply - strong risk-asset headwind
trading.interpretation.mvrvDeepBuy: Deep buy zone - historically rare undervaluation
trading.interpretation.realizedBelowPric: Below realized price - market at loss, strong accumulation signal
trading.projection.description.balancedP: Delta Cap / Realized Cap model floor
trading.projection.description.realizedP: Average cost basis of all coins
trading.projection.description.volatilit: One standard deviation below the 365-day mean
One standard deviation above the 365-day mean
trading.projection.description.athRetest: Retest of the highest stored daily close
trading.projection.description.terminalP: Upper bound fair value from realized cap
trading.projection.description.ath1272: First breakout extension above prior all-time high
trading.projection.description.volatilit: Two standard deviations above the 365-day mean
trading.projection.description.ath1618: Golden-ratio extension above prior all-time high
trading.projection.description.ath2: Prior high doubling scenario for euphoric cycles
trading.projection.description.cycleHeat: Current price extended by cycle heat from 12m RSI and Rainbow band
trading.signalInfo.current: Current value is {$PH} and it is classified as {$PH_1}.
trading.signalInfo.fallback: This signal contributes to the market score using the current value and its historical bullish or bearish zone.
trading.signalInfo.mvrv: MVRV compares market value with realized value to show whether BTC is priced above or below aggregate on-chain cost basis. Range guide: below 0 is deep undervaluation, 0-2 is accumulation, 2-4 is fair to warm, 4-7 is elevated, and above 7 is overheated.
trading.signalInfo.fearGreed: The Fear & Greed Index measures market sentiment from 0 to 100. Range guide: 0-20 is extreme fear, 20-40 fear, 40-60 neutral, 60-80 greed, and 80-100 extreme greed. Low readings are often contrarian bullish; very high readings can warn of crowding.
trading.signalInfo.rainbowBand: The Rainbow Band places price in long-term logarithmic valuation bands. Range guide: lower bands are cheaper accumulation zones, middle bands are neutral, and upper bands signal overheated or bubble-like conditions.
trading.signalInfo.realizedPrice: Realized Price Premium shows BTC price versus realized price, the aggregate on-chain cost basis. Range guide: below 0% means price is below cost basis, 0-30% is a healthy premium, 30-100% is moderate, 100-250% is high, and above 250% is extreme.
trading.signalInfo.realizedPricePremium: Realized Price Premium shows BTC price versus realized price, the aggregate on-chain cost basis. Range guide: below 0% means price is below cost basis, 0-30% is a healthy premium, 30-100% is moderate, 100-250% is high, and above 250% is extreme.
trading.signalInfo.nupl: NUPL estimates market-wide unrealized profit or loss. Range guide: below 0 is capitulation, 0-25% is hope or fear, 25-50% optimism, 50-75% belief, and above 75% euphoria.
trading.signalInfo.vdd: VDD Multiple tracks value-days destroyed against its normal level, highlighting older coins moving on-chain. Range guide: below 0.5 is quiet accumulation, 0.5-1 bullish, 1-3 normal, 3-6 caution, and above 6 distribution risk.
trading.signalInfo.piCycle: Pi Cycle Top measures the gap between the 111-day moving average and twice the 350-day moving average. Range guide: a large positive gap means the market is far from the historical top crossover, near 0 means top risk is rising, and below 0 means the crossover has occurred.
trading.signalInfo.piCycleTop: Pi Cycle Top measures the gap between the 111-day moving average and twice the 350-day moving average. Range guide: a large positive gap means the market is far from the historical top crossover, near 0 means top risk is rising, and below 0 means the crossover has occurred.
trading.signalInfo.mayer: Mayer Multiple is BTC price divided by the 200-day moving average. Range guide: below 0.8 is deep accumulation, 0.8-1 is accumulation, 1-1.5 is healthy trend, 1.5-2.4 is extended, and above 2.4 is overheated.
trading.signalInfo.puell: Puell Multiple compares miner revenue with its 365-day average. Range guide: below 0.5 is a rare buy zone, 0.5-1 accumulation, 1-4 normal, 4-8 late-cycle caution, and above 8 overheated miner-revenue conditions.
trading.signalInfo.s2f: S2F Model Premium compares spot price with the Stock-to-Flow model price. Positive values mean spot is below the model price, while negative values mean spot is above it. Large positive discounts are bullish; large negative premiums are riskier.
trading.signalInfo.projectionRange: Projection Range shows where BTC sits between modeled downside floors and upper terminal-style targets. Range guide: near 0% is close to modeled floor, 25-60% is mid-range, 60-90% is upper range, and above 90% is near or beyond modeled terminal levels.
trading.signalInfo.volatilityPosition: 365d Volatility Position shows price relative to its 365-day trend band as a z-score. Range guide: below -1.5 is deep discount, -1.5 to -0.5 accumulation, -0.5 to 0.75 normal, 0.75 to 1.5 extended, and above 1.5 overheated.
trading.signalInfo.globalM2: Global M2 YoY tracks broad liquidity growth. Range guide: negative growth is a macro headwind, 0-5% is mild, 5-10% is supportive, and above 10% is a strong liquidity tailwind for risk assets.
trading.signalInfo.dxy: DXY YoY tracks the yearly change in the US Dollar Index. Falling DXY is usually a tailwind for BTC, stable DXY is neutral, and rising DXY is a headwind. Rough guide: below -3% supports risk assets, -3% to 3% is neutral, and above 3% is a headwind.
trading.signalInfo.exchangeNetflow: Exchange Netflow measures BTC moving into or out of exchanges. Negative values mean net outflows and often lower sell-side supply; positive values mean inflows and possible sell pressure. Large outflows are bullish, large inflows are bearish.
trading.signalInfo.fundingRate: Funding Rate shows the average perpetual-swap payment between longs and shorts. Negative funding often means shorts are crowded and can be contrarian bullish, near zero is neutral, and high positive funding means crowded longs and higher liquidation risk.
trading.signalInfo.openInterest: Open Interest measures the size of outstanding derivatives positions. Lower leverage load is healthier and less fragile; very high open interest can make price more sensitive to liquidations and sharp reversals.
trading.signalInfo.soprSplit: LTH/STH SOPR compares whether long-term and short-term holders are spending coins in profit or loss. Around 1 is breakeven, below 1 suggests loss realization and reset, and high values suggest profit-taking or distribution.
trading.signalInfo.googleTrends: Google Trends measures public search interest for Bitcoin on a 0-100 scale. Low attention can support accumulation, rising moderate attention can confirm demand, and extreme attention can mark overheated retail interest.
trading.signalInfo.activeAddresses: Active Addresses counts daily unique addresses active on the Bitcoin network. Higher usage helps confirm organic demand; weak activity means price strength has less network-use confirmation.
trading.signalInfo.btcDvol: BTC DVOL is implied volatility from options markets. Range guide: below 45 is calm, 45-70 normal, 70-95 elevated, and above 95 stress or very high expected movement.
trading.signalInfo.excessLiquidity: Excess Liquidity estimates the leading macro liquidity impulse after accounting for dollar pressure. Positive values are a tailwind for BTC, near zero is neutral, and negative values are a drag on risk appetite.

charts.compareBullMarkets.title: Compare Bull Markets
charts.compareBullMarkets.subtitle: Historical Bitcoin bull-market breakouts scaled to the current reward era
charts.compareBullMarkets.yAxis: Bitcoin price scaled to current reward era
charts.compareBullMarkets.about: Compare Bull Markets aligns Bitcoin bull-market breakouts by days since each era first breaks above the previous all-time high. Earlier eras are scaled to the current reward era breakout price, which makes historical percentage moves easier to compare against today.
charts.compareBullMarkets.interpretation: A breakout starts when price moves above the previous bull-market high. Each line then continues through the available data for that reward era, so prior cycles remain visible after their main ATH drawdown.

faq.title: How can we help?
faq.subtitle: Search questions and answers about every feature of the platform.
faq.noResults: No answers found for your search.
faq.a.whatIs: BitWLab is a free Bitcoin on-chain analytics platform. It brings together over 20 valuation models, cycle indicators, and macro context charts in one focused workspace — no subscriptions or paid data feeds required.
faq.a.isFree: Yes, completely. All charts, models, alerts, and tools are free with no premium tiers or paywalls. The platform relies entirely on publicly available data sources.
faq.q.needAccount: Do I need an account to use BitWLab?
faq.a.needAccount: Yes. A free account is required to access live charts and features. Registration only needs your email address — no credit card or personal data beyond that.
faq.a.languages: The platform supports English and Hungarian. Switch at any time using the EN / HU toggle in the top navigation bar, or permanently from My Account in the user menu.
faq.a.dashboard: The Dashboard is your personal overview screen. It shows customisable metric widgets for key Bitcoin indicators, a market signals banner with a composite cycle score, and quick access to your favourite and recently viewed charts.
faq.a.widgets: Widgets are metric cards arranged in a grid on your Dashboard. Each widget shows a key value — such as MVRV Z-Score, BTC 24-hour change, Stock-to-Flow, Halving progress, or Fear & Greed index — updated automatically.
faq.q.addWidget: How do I add or remove a widget?
faq.a.addWidget: Click “Add Widget” at the top of the Dashboard to browse available metrics and add them to your grid. To remove a widget, hover over it and click the ✕ button that appears in the corner. Drag widgets to reorder them.
faq.q.signalsBanner: What is the Market Signals Banner?
faq.a.signalsBanner: The banner at the top of the Dashboard shows a composite cycle score (−100 to +100), the current BTC price, an overall zone label (Very Bullish to Very Bearish), and individual readings for each tracked indicator. Clicking it opens the Trade Planner.
faq.a.chartsCount: Over 20 analytical models are available: MVRV Z-Score, Rainbow Band, Pi Cycle Top, Stock-to-Flow, Fear & Greed, Puell Multiple, Power Law, CVDD, Halving Spiral, Realized Price, 200-Day MA, 2-Year MA Multiplier, Hash Ribbons, VDD Multiple, Mayer Multiple, Difficulty Ribbon, 200-Week MA Heatmap, NVT Ratio, Thermocap Multiple, Excess Liquidity, and S&P 500 Liquidity.
faq.q.navigate: How do I browse the chart library?
faq.a.navigate: Click “Charts” in the top navigation bar to open the Chart Library. Charts are grouped by category and searchable by name. Click any chart card to open the full interactive chart page.
faq.a.export: Yes. On any chart page use the export menu in the toolbar to download the chart as a PNG image or CSV data file. The export captures all visible data and annotations.
faq.a.annotations: Annotations let you place personal notes on a chart at specific dates. Click the annotation tool in the chart toolbar, then click the chart at the date you want to mark. Your notes are saved to your account and persist across sessions.
faq.q.favourites: How do I save a chart to my favourites?
faq.a.favourites: Click the star (★) icon on any chart page to add it to your favourites. Starred charts appear in the Favourites tab on your Dashboard for quick access. Click the star again to remove it.
faq.a.tradePlanner: The Trade Planner is a decision-support tool that aggregates multiple on-chain cycle signals into a composite score. It shows a signal grid, price projections based on historical cycle data, and a personal trade plan log where you can record entries, targets, and notes.
faq.q.scoreCalc: How is the market signal score calculated?
faq.a.scoreCalc: The composite score (−100 to +100) aggregates readings from MVRV Z-Score, Rainbow Band, Fear & Greed, Stock-to-Flow, Puell Multiple, and other indicators. Each contributes a normalised sub-score based on its current zone. Higher positive scores indicate bullish cycle conditions; lower negative scores indicate bearish conditions.
faq.q.customizeSignals: Can I choose which signals count toward my score?
faq.a.customizeSignals: Yes. On the Market Signals tab, click the gear "Configure" button next to "Signals used in your score" to open the signal picker. Check or uncheck any indicator to include or exclude it from your composite score — hover or focus a signal in the dialog to read its explanation. At least one signal must stay selected, and your choices are saved automatically.
faq.a.alerts: Alerts are email notifications triggered when a chosen indicator crosses a threshold you set. For example, get notified when the MVRV Z-Score exceeds 7, or when the Fear & Greed index drops below 20.
faq.q.createAlert: How do I create an alert?
faq.a.createAlert: Go to the Alerts page from the top navigation, click “Create alert”, select an indicator and metric, set a threshold direction (above or below) and value, then save. You will receive an email when the condition is met. Alerts can be paused, edited, or deleted at any time.
faq.q.changeLanguage: How do I change the interface language?
faq.a.changeLanguage: Click EN or HU in the top navigation bar to switch between English and Hungarian. To save your preference permanently, open My Account from the user menu, select a language, and click Save changes.
faq.q.updateProfile: How do I update my name or preferences?
faq.a.updateProfile: Click your avatar (initials button) in the top-right corner and select “My Account”. Update your full name and language preference, then click “Save changes” to apply.
faq.q.dataCollect: What data does BitWLab collect about me?
faq.a.dataCollect: Only your email address and optionally your full name are stored. Standard server logs (IP address, browser type) are kept for security. No payment data is collected and no third-party tracking cookies are used. See Privacy Policy in the user menu for full details.
dashboard.noFavourites: No favourite charts yet. Open a chart and click {$START_TAG_STRONG}Save{$CLOSE_TAG_STRONG} to bookmark it.
landing.platformTitle: A focused Bitcoin research workspace for everyday market analysis
landing.functionChartsCopy: Explore valuation models, cycle indicators, moving averages, and macro context in one chart library.
landing.functionWorkspaceCopy: Save dashboards, follow recent charts, and organize the signals that matter to your research.
landing.functionWorkflowCopy: Use alerts, trading plans, exports, and admin tools without subscriptions or paid data feeds.
landing.model.soprRatio.tooltip: Compares long-term and short-term holder profit-taking behavior through the LTH/STH SOPR ratio.
landing.model.compareBullMarkets.tooltip: Aligns Bitcoin bull-market breakouts by days since the prior all-time high and scales previous eras to the current reward era.
landing.model.exchangeReserve.tooltip: Tracks the total BTC held on exchanges. Rising reserves imply more coins available for sale (bearish); falling reserves imply accumulation and increasing scarcity (bullish).
landing.riskNotice: Not financial advice. Cryptocurrency investments carry risk.
disclaimer.title: Disclaimer: not financial advice
disclaimer.intro: The information, data, charts, analysis, metrics, indicators, and blockchain-related content on this platform are provided for informational and educational purposes only.
disclaimer.notAdvisor: This platform is not a financial advisor, investment advisor, broker, dealer, or financial institution. Nothing displayed on this website constitutes financial advice, investment advice, trading advice, legal advice, tax advice, or any recommendation to buy, sell, hold, or trade any cryptocurrency, digital asset, token, security, or financial instrument.
disclaimer.dataSources: All information presented on this platform is derived from publicly available blockchain data, third-party data providers, or automated analytical processes. The platform merely aggregates, visualizes, and presents blockchain information and current network activity through charts, dashboards, statistics, and other analytical tools.
disclaimer.historicalData: The charts, indicators, rankings, wallet information, transaction data, market metrics, and other visualizations displayed on this website reflect historical or real-time blockchain data and should not be interpreted as predictions, guarantees, recommendations, or endorsements of future performance.
disclaimer.volatility: Cryptocurrency and digital asset markets are highly volatile and involve substantial risk. Users are solely responsible for conducting their own research and due diligence before making any financial, investment, or trading decisions. Any actions taken based on information obtained from this platform are done entirely at the user's own risk.
disclaimer.accuracy: While we strive to provide accurate and up-to-date information, we make no representations or warranties regarding the accuracy, completeness, reliability, availability, or timeliness of any information displayed. Blockchain data may be delayed, incomplete, inaccurate, or subject to technical limitations.
disclaimer.acknowledgementIntro: By using this platform, you acknowledge and agree that:
disclaimer.ack1: The platform provides informational blockchain data only.
disclaimer.ack2: No financial, investment, legal, or tax advice is being provided.
disclaimer.ack3: You are solely responsible for your own investment and trading decisions.
disclaimer.ack4: The platform and its operators shall not be liable for any losses, damages, or consequences arising from the use of the information provided.
disclaimer.consult: Always consult qualified financial, legal, and tax professionals before making any investment decisions.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.s1.title: 1. What we collect
privacy.s1.body: When you create an account we collect your email address and, if provided, your full name. During normal use we collect standard server logs (IP address, browser type, pages visited) for security and performance purposes. We do not collect payment information.
privacy.s2.title: 2. How we use your data
privacy.s2.body: Your data is used solely to operate the service: authenticate your account, deliver email notifications and alerts you have configured, and improve the platform. We do not sell, rent, or share your personal data with third parties for marketing purposes.
privacy.s3.title: 3. Data storage and security
privacy.s3.body: Data is stored on servers within the European Union. We use industry-standard encryption (TLS) for data in transit and apply access controls to data at rest. Passwords are stored as one-way cryptographic hashes and are never stored in plain text.
privacy.s4.title: 4. Cookies and local storage
privacy.s4.body: We use a single session cookie to keep you logged in and browser local storage to remember your language preference and dashboard layout. No third-party tracking cookies are used.
privacy.s5.title: 5. Your rights
privacy.s5.body: You may request access to, correction of, or deletion of your personal data at any time by contacting us. Accounts can be deleted on request. We will respond within 30 days.
privacy.s6.title: 6. Changes to this policy
privacy.s6.body: We may update this policy from time to time. Material changes will be communicated via the platform or email. Continued use of the service after changes constitutes acceptance.
privacy.s7.title: 7. Contact
privacy.s7.body: Questions about this policy can be directed to the platform operator via the contact information provided on the platform.
terms.title: Terms of use
terms.s1.title: 1. Acceptance of terms
terms.s1.body: By registering for or using BitWLab you agree to these Terms of Use in full. If you do not agree you must not use the platform. You must be at least 18 years of age to use this service.
terms.s2.title: 2. Description of service
terms.s2.body: BitWLab provides Bitcoin on-chain analytics, valuation model charts, cycle indicators, and related informational tools. All content is provided for informational purposes only and does not constitute financial advice. See the full Disclaimer for details.
terms.s3.title: 3. Your account
terms.s3.body: You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your login credentials and for all activity that occurs under your account. You must notify us immediately of any unauthorised use of your account.
terms.s4.title: 4. Acceptable use
terms.s4.body: You agree not to: attempt to reverse-engineer, scrape, or systematically extract data from the platform; interfere with or disrupt the service or its servers; use the service for any unlawful purpose; or impersonate any person or entity.
terms.s5.title: 5. Intellectual property
terms.s5.body: All platform content, design, code, and branding are the property of the platform operator and are protected by applicable intellectual-property laws. You may not copy, reproduce, or redistribute any part of the platform without prior written permission.
terms.s6.title: 6. Disclaimer of warranties
terms.s6.body: The service is provided "as is" and "as available" without warranties of any kind, either express or implied. We do not warrant that the service will be uninterrupted, error-free, or that data will be accurate, complete, or current at all times.
terms.s7.title: 7. Limitation of liability
terms.s7.body: To the fullest extent permitted by law, the platform operator shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including loss of profits or data, arising from your use of the service or reliance on any information provided.
terms.s8.title: 8. Termination
terms.s8.body: We reserve the right to suspend or terminate your account at any time if you violate these terms or for any other reason at our sole discretion, with or without notice.
terms.s9.title: 9. Governing law
terms.s9.body: These terms are governed by the laws of Hungary. Any disputes shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Hungary.
terms.s10.title: 10. Changes to terms
terms.s10.body: We may revise these terms at any time. The updated version will be posted on this page with a new effective date. Continued use of the service after changes constitutes your acceptance of the revised terms.

charts.library.midterm-cycles.title: Midterm Cycles
charts.library.midterm-cycles.signal: BTC & SPX RSI vs election cycles
charts.library.midterm-cycles.descriptio: Overlays Bitcoin and S&P 500 12-month RSI against the Chicago Fed National Activity Index (CFNAI), aligned to US midterm election cycles. Historically, equity and economic conditions around midterms have preceded significant Bitcoin moves.
charts.midtermCycles.title: Midterm Cycles
charts.midtermCycles.subtitle: BTC & SPX 12-month RSI vs CFNAI, aligned to US midterm election cycles
charts.midtermCycles.about: Overlays Bitcoin and S&P 500 12-month RSI (Relative Strength Index) against the Chicago Fed National Activity Index (CFNAI), aligned to US midterm election cycles. CFNAI is a composite of 85 monthly indicators — positive values indicate above-trend growth, negative values indicate below-trend growth. Data: FRED (free, no API key required) + on-chain BTC prices.
charts.midtermCycles.overbought: BTC RSI above 70 — historically elevated, potential cycle top territory.
charts.midtermCycles.oversold: BTC RSI below 40 — historically oversold, potential accumulation zone.
charts.empty.no.data.yet.historical.data: No data yet. Historical data will appear after the first refresh.
landing.freePromiseBody: Every chart, alert, dashboard, and research tool is available without subscriptions, premium tiers, or paywalls.
landing.dataPromiseBody: BitWLab uses publicly available and no-cost data sources, so the analysis stays transparent and accessible.
auth.loginSubtitle: Sign in to your account to continue
footer.disclaimer: Not financial advice. Cryptocurrency investments carry risk.
auth.registerSubtitle: Fill in your details to get started
charts.nvt.about: The NVT Ratio (Network Value to Transactions) is Bitcoin's equivalent of a P/E ratio: market cap divided by daily on-chain transaction volume. The NVT Signal uses a 90-day moving average of NVT to reduce noise. Values above 150 historically signal overvaluation relative to network usage; values below 45 signal undervaluation.
charts.nvt.interpretation.waiting: NVT Signal not yet available (requires sufficient transaction volume history).
charts.nvt.interpretation.overvalued: NVT Signal is {$PH}, above the overvaluation threshold of 150. This suggests that Bitcoin's network value is high relative to on-chain transaction volume, historically a signal of overvaluation.
charts.nvt.interpretation.undervalued: NVT Signal is {$PH}, below the undervaluation threshold of 45. This suggests Bitcoin's network is being heavily utilised relative to its market cap, historically a signal of undervaluation.
charts.nvt.interpretation.neutral: NVT Signal is {$PH}, within the neutral range (45–150). Network usage appears proportionate to Bitcoin's current market cap.
charts.priceForecast.about: Price Forecast Tools combines on-chain models to identify historically reliable price targets. Top Cap and Delta Top have tracked Bitcoin's cycle highs; CVDD has tracked major bear-market lows.
charts.priceForecast.dataSource.btc: BTC Price & Supply: CoinGecko + halving schedule (stored daily)
charts.priceForecast.dataSource.cap: Top Cap & Delta Top: Computed from Average Cap and Realized Cap — full history
charts.priceForecast.dataSource.cvdd: CVDD: Power-law model shared with the Bitcoin CVDD chart
charts.priceForecast.interpretation.cvdd: BTC price ({$PH}) is below CVDD ({$PH_1}). Historically this is a deep undervaluation zone that coincides with bear market lows.
charts.priceForecast.interpretation.topC: BTC price ({$PH}) is above Top Cap ({$PH_1}). This has historically marked cycle tops.
charts.priceForecast.interpretation.delt: BTC price ({$PH}) is above Delta Top ({$PH_1}), approaching the upper range of historical cycle peaks.
charts.priceForecast.interpretation.neut: BTC price ({$PH}) is in the neutral range between bear market floor models and cycle top targets.
charts.soprRatio.about: The SOPR Ratio divides Long-Term Holder SOPR by Short-Term Holder SOPR. It compares profit-taking behavior between experienced holders and newer market participants.
charts.soprRatio.interpretation.waiting: Waiting for the latest SOPR Ratio data.
charts.soprRatio.interpretation.high: The SOPR Ratio is above 1. Long-term holders are realizing relatively more profit than short-term holders, a pattern often seen as experienced holders distribute into strength.
charts.soprRatio.interpretation.low: The SOPR Ratio is below 1. Short-term holders are realizing relatively more profit than long-term holders, which is common during early bull phases or short-term momentum bursts.
charts.soprRatio.interpretation.neutral: The SOPR Ratio is near 1, meaning long-term and short-term holders are realizing profits or losses at similar relative rates.
charts.soprRatio.dataSource.price: Bitcoin price: CoinGecko API via backend price history
charts.soprRatio.dataSource.lth: LTH SOPR: bitcoin-data.com free public API (available from 25 June 2022)
charts.soprRatio.dataSource.sth: STH SOPR: bitcoin-data.com free public API (available from 25 June 2022)
charts.soprRatio.dataSource.calculation: Calculation: LTH SOPR divided by STH SOPR, with 7-day and 30-day moving averages computed in the browser
charts.mvrv.about: The MVRV Z-Score compares Bitcoin's market capitalization to its realized capitalization, then normalizes the difference using standard deviation. Values above 7 historically signal major cycle tops; negative values have marked generational buying opportunities.
charts.mvrv.interpretation.waiting: Waiting for the latest MVRV Z-Score data.
charts.mvrv.interpretation.overheated: The MVRV Z-Score is in the overheated zone (above 7). Historically, readings above 7 have coincided with major cycle tops. Exercise caution.
charts.mvrv.interpretation.elevated: The MVRV Z-Score is elevated (5-7), suggesting the market is overextended relative to realized value. Historically associated with late-cycle conditions.
charts.mvrv.interpretation.caution: The MVRV Z-Score is in the caution zone (3-5). The market is pricing in a moderate premium over realized value.
charts.mvrv.interpretation.fairValue: The MVRV Z-Score is in the fair value zone (0-3). Bitcoin is trading near or below historical mean valuations relative to realized cap.
charts.mvrv.interpretation.undervalued: The MVRV Z-Score is negative - Bitcoin is trading below its realized value on a statistical basis. Historically this has coincided with strong long-term accumulation opportunities.
charts.mvrv.dataSource.price: Bitcoin price: CoinGecko via backend DB (full history)
charts.mvrv.dataSource.realizedProxy: Realized price (pre-June 2022): 5-year EMA proxy - approximation of long-term cost basis
charts.mvrv.dataSource.formula: MVRV Z-Score: ln(price / realized) x 4, calibrated to match traditional cycle-top scale
charts.powerLaw.about: The Bitcoin Power Law Chart models Bitcoin's price as a power function of time since genesis (Jan 3, 2009). Logarithmic regression produces a long-term fair value (Model) line with upper (Ceiling) and lower (Floor) price bounds that have historically contained Bitcoin's price through multiple market cycles.
charts.powerLaw.dataSource.model: Model: power law regression - log10(Price) = 5.845 x log10(days since genesis) - 17.016
charts.powerLaw.dataSource.bounds: Floor / Ceiling: x0.25 below / x10 above model price
charts.powerLaw.interpretation.waiting: Waiting for the latest price data.
charts.powerLaw.interpretation.belowFloo: Bitcoin is below the Power Law floor - a historically rare and extremely undervalued condition.
charts.powerLaw.interpretation.lowerRang: Bitcoin is trading in the lower range between the floor and model, suggesting historically undervalued conditions.
charts.powerLaw.interpretation.belowMode: Bitcoin is trading below the model price, historically associated with accumulation phases.
charts.powerLaw.interpretation.nearModel: Bitcoin is trading near the model price - close to long-term fair value based on the power law.
charts.powerLaw.interpretation.elevated: Bitcoin is trading above the model price, entering elevated territory relative to the long-term trend.
charts.powerLaw.interpretation.nearCeili: Bitcoin is approaching the Power Law ceiling, historically associated with cycle-top conditions.
charts.powerLaw.interpretation.aboveCeil: Bitcoin is above the Power Law ceiling - a historically rare event linked to speculative peak conditions.
charts.realizedPrice.about: Bitcoin Realized Price values each coin at the price when it last moved on-chain, then divides that value by circulating supply. This gives an estimate of the market's aggregate cost basis. When BTC price is above realized price, holders are in profit on aggregate; when BTC price is below realized price, the market is carrying aggregate paper losses.
charts.realizedPrice.interpretation.wait: Waiting for realized price data to compute the MVRV ratio.
charts.realizedPrice.interpretation.over: MVRV Ratio is {$INTERPOLATION}. Market price is far above realized price, so aggregate holders are deeply in profit; historically this has appeared in late-cycle conditions.
charts.realizedPrice.interpretation.elev: MVRV Ratio is {$INTERPOLATION}. Price is meaningfully above the aggregate on-chain cost basis, so most market participants are holding paper profits.
charts.realizedPrice.interpretation.fair: MVRV Ratio is {$INTERPOLATION}. BTC trades above realized price, meaning the market is in aggregate profit but still close to its on-chain cost basis.
charts.realizedPrice.interpretation.stre: MVRV Ratio is {$INTERPOLATION}. BTC trades below realized price, meaning aggregate holders are underwater; historically these periods have clustered near major cycle lows.
charts.realizedPrice.dataSource.price: Bitcoin price: CoinGecko API via backend price history
charts.realizedPrice.dataSource.realized: Realized price: stored DB values plus CoinMetrics community API history derived from PriceUSD / CapMVRVCur
charts.realizedPrice.dataSource.mvrv: MVRV Ratio: BTC price divided by realized price, computed by the backend
charts.stockToFlow.about: The Bitcoin Stock-to-Flow model estimates Bitcoin value from scarcity, using the ratio between existing supply and new issuance. It is most useful as long-term historical context around halving cycles.
charts.stockToFlow.interpretation.waitin: Waiting for the latest Stock-to-Flow model calculation.
charts.stockToFlow.interpretation.overva: Bitcoin is trading materially above the Stock-to-Flow model price. Treat this as historical context rather than a standalone sell signal.
charts.stockToFlow.interpretation.underv: Bitcoin is trading materially below the Stock-to-Flow model price. Historically, this has marked periods where price lagged scarcity-model expectations.
charts.stockToFlow.interpretation.neutra: Bitcoin is trading near the Stock-to-Flow model price. The model is best used as long-term scarcity context, not a precise forecast.
charts.stockToFlow.dataSource.supply: Supply schedule: Bitcoin issuance and halving schedule
charts.stockToFlow.dataSource.calculatio: Calculation: Stock-to-Flow ratio and model price calculated from Bitcoin supply and issuance flow
charts.thermocap.title: Thermocap Multiple
charts.thermocap.subtitle: Bitcoin market cap relative to cumulative miner revenue
charts.thermocap.about: The Thermocap Multiple measures Bitcoin's market capitalisation relative to its Thermocap — the cumulative total of all miner revenues in USD, representing the total capital invested to secure the network. Historically, multiples above 33× have marked cycle tops while values below 4× have indicated deep value accumulation zones.
charts.thermocap.interpretation.overvalu: The Thermocap Multiple is {$PH}×, above the historical overvaluation threshold of 33×. This level has historically coincided with cycle tops and suggests elevated risk.
charts.thermocap.interpretation.elevated: The Thermocap Multiple is {$PH}×, in the elevated range (above 10×). The market is heating up but has not yet reached historically extreme levels.
charts.thermocap.interpretation.accumula: The Thermocap Multiple is {$PH}×, below the accumulation threshold of 4×. Historically this has been a deep value zone offering strong long-term entry opportunities.
charts.thermocap.interpretation.neutral: The Thermocap Multiple is {$PH}×, in the neutral range between 4× and 10×. The market is within historically normal bounds without signalling either extreme.
charts.signal.piCycle.topSignal: Pi Cycle Top signal (111-day MA above 350-day MA × 2)
charts.nupl.interpretation.waiting: Waiting for realized price data to compute NUPL.
charts.nupl.interpretation.euphoria: NUPL is in the Euphoria / Greed zone. Historically, major Bitcoin cycle peaks have occurred after the market entered this band.
charts.nupl.interpretation.belief: NUPL is in the Belief / Denial zone. Aggregate holders are strongly in profit, which often appears in mature bull-market conditions.
charts.nupl.interpretation.optimism: NUPL is in the Optimism / Anxiety zone. The market is profitable on aggregate but not yet in historical euphoria.
charts.nupl.interpretation.hope: NUPL is in the Hope / Fear zone. The market is close to its aggregate cost basis, a transition area between stress and recovery.
charts.nupl.interpretation.capitulation: NUPL is in the Capitulation zone. Historically, negative readings have clustered near major Bitcoin cycle lows.
charts.nupl.about: Net Unrealized Profit/Loss (NUPL), also called Relative Unrealized Profit/Loss, compares Bitcoin's market value with realized value. Market value is current price multiplied by circulating supply. Realized value prices each coin where it last moved on-chain, averages those coin prices, then multiplies by circulating supply. Subtracting realized value from market value estimates aggregate paper profit or loss. Dividing that by market value creates NUPL, a sentiment gauge that maps Bitcoin cycle phases from Capitulation through Euphoria / Greed.
charts.nupl.dataSource.price: Bitcoin price: CoinGecko API via backend price history
charts.nupl.dataSource.realized: Realized price: backend realized price series derived from stored DB values and CoinMetrics community API history
charts.nupl.dataSource.formula: NUPL: (BTC price - realized price) / BTC price
charts.hashRateSubtitle: Total network computational power — a raw miner-investment and network-security trend
charts.hashRateLoadFailed: Chart data could not be loaded. Please try again.
charts.hashRate.noData: Hash rate data is not available yet.
charts.hashRate.about: Hash Rate measures the total computational power securing the Bitcoin network. It complements the existing Hash Ribbons and Puell Multiple charts (both derived from this same series) by showing the raw underlying trend rather than a moving-average crossover or a revenue ratio. A steadily rising hash rate reflects growing miner investment and network security; sharp drops typically mark miner capitulation events.
charts.hashRate.interpretation.rising: Hash rate has risen {$INTERPOLATION} over the past 30 days. A rising hash rate reflects growing miner investment and network security, historically associated with long-term confidence in the network.
charts.hashRate.interpretation.falling: Hash rate has fallen {$INTERPOLATION} over the past 30 days. A falling hash rate can reflect miner capitulation — unprofitable miners powering down — often coinciding with local price bottoms once the weakest miners have exited.
charts.hashRate.interpretation.stable: Hash rate has been roughly stable over the past 30 days, suggesting no major shift in miner participation.
charts.hashRate.dataSource.price: Bitcoin price: CoinGecko API via backend price history
charts.hashRate.dataSource.hashRate: Hash rate: Blockchain.info charts API — full daily history since 2009
charts.library.hash-rate.title: Bitcoin: Hash Rate
charts.library.hash-rate.signal: Miner investment and network security trend
charts.library.hash-rate.description: Total network computational power, the raw series behind the existing Hash Ribbons and Puell Multiple charts. A rising trend reflects growing miner investment and security; sharp drops mark miner capitulation events.
landing.model.hashRate.tooltip: Total network computational power — a raw miner-investment and network-security trend, complementing Hash Ribbons and Puell Multiple.
charts.activeAddressesSubtitle: Unique daily active addresses — a fundamentals-based network usage and adoption trend
charts.activeAddressesLoadFailed: Chart data could not be loaded. Please try again.
charts.activeAddresses.noData: Active addresses data is not available yet.
charts.activeAddresses.about: Active Addresses counts the number of unique Bitcoin addresses that sent or received a transaction each day. It is a fundamentals-based measure of network usage and adoption, independent of the valuation-ratio charts (MVRV, NVT, etc.) elsewhere in this library. Sustained growth reflects expanding real usage; a stalling or falling count while price keeps rising is a classic divergence warning seen near past cycle tops.
charts.activeAddresses.interpretation.ri: Active addresses have risen {$INTERPOLATION} over the past 30 days. Growing network usage historically accompanies healthy cycle expansion.
charts.activeAddresses.interpretation.fa: Active addresses have fallen {$INTERPOLATION} over the past 30 days. Declining network usage — especially alongside a rising price — is a classic late-cycle divergence warning.
charts.activeAddresses.interpretation.st: Active addresses have been roughly stable over the past 30 days, suggesting no major shift in network usage.
charts.activeAddresses.dataSource.price: Bitcoin price: CoinGecko API via backend price history
charts.activeAddresses.dataSource.count: Active addresses: CoinMetrics Community API (AdrActCnt) — full daily history since 2009
charts.library.active-addresses.title: Bitcoin: Active Addresses
charts.library.active-addresses.descript: Unique daily active addresses, a fundamentals-based measure of network usage independent of the valuation-ratio charts elsewhere in this library. Falling usage while price rises is a classic late-cycle divergence warning.
landing.model.activeAddresses.tooltip: Unique daily active addresses — a fundamentals-based network usage trend, independent of the valuation-ratio charts elsewhere in this library.
charts.realizedVolatilitySubtitle: Annualized 30-day / 90-day price volatility — spotting compression before big moves
charts.realizedVolatilityLoadFailed: Chart data could not be loaded. Please try again.
charts.realizedVolatility.noData: Realized volatility data is not available yet.
charts.realizedVolatility.about: Realized Volatility measures how much BTC's price has actually swung, expressed as an annualized standard deviation of daily log returns over trailing 30-day and 90-day windows. Unlike the valuation-ratio charts elsewhere in this library, volatility is not directional — both cycle tops and capitulation bottoms show elevated readings. Its main use is spotting compression (unusually low volatility), which has historically preceded large moves in either direction.
charts.realizedVolatility.interpretation: 30-day realized volatility is {$INTERPOLATION}, a low reading for Bitcoin. Volatility compression like this has historically preceded large moves in either direction — it is a "get ready" signal, not a directional one.

30-day realized volatility is {$INTERPOLATION}, a high reading for Bitcoin. Elevated volatility marks turbulent conditions and shows up at both capitulation bottoms and euphoric tops — check price trend and other charts in this library to tell which.
30-day realized volatility is {$INTERPOLATION}, within Bitcoin's typical historical range.
charts.realizedVolatility.dataSource.pri: Bitcoin price: CoinGecko API via backend price history. Volatility is computed entirely from this existing price series — no separate external data source is required.
charts.library.realized-volatility.title: Bitcoin: Realized Volatility
charts.library.realized-volatility.descr: Annualized 30-day and 90-day realized volatility, computed from BTC's own price history. Not directional — both tops and bottoms show elevated readings — but volatility compression has historically preceded large moves in either direction.
landing.model.realizedVolatility.tooltip: Annualized 30-day / 90-day price volatility computed from BTC's own price history — volatility compression has historically preceded large moves in either direction.
charts.btcDvolSubtitle: Deribit's options-implied volatility index — forward-looking, history since 2021 only
charts.btcDvolLoadFailed: Chart data could not be loaded. Please try again.
charts.btcDvol.noData: DVOL data is not available yet.
charts.btcDvol.about: DVOL is Deribit's BTC implied volatility index, derived from the prices of BTC options — it reflects what options markets expect volatility to be over the coming 30 days, forward-looking rather than the backward-looking Realized Volatility chart elsewhere in this library. Like realized volatility, DVOL is not directional: both capitulation bottoms and euphoric tops show elevated readings. Note: DVOL history only goes back to Deribit's index launch on 2021-03-24, far shorter than most other charts in this library.
charts.btcDvol.interpretation.low: DVOL is {$INTERPOLATION}, a low reading. Options markets are pricing in a calm near-term outlook — historically a precursor to larger moves once that complacency breaks.
charts.btcDvol.interpretation.high: DVOL is {$INTERPOLATION}, a high reading. Options markets are pricing in significant near-term turbulence — seen at both panic-driven bottoms and euphoric blow-off tops.
charts.btcDvol.interpretation.normal: DVOL is {$INTERPOLATION}, within its typical range.
charts.btcDvol.dataSource.price: Bitcoin price: CoinGecko API via backend price history
charts.btcDvol.dataSource.dvol: Implied volatility: Deribit public API (get_volatility_index_data) — daily BTC DVOL index, history since 2021-03-24 only
charts.library.btc-dvol.title: Bitcoin: Implied Volatility (DVOL)
charts.library.btc-dvol.description: Deribit's BTC implied volatility index, derived from options prices — a forward-looking counterpart to Realized Volatility. Note: history only goes back to Deribit's index launch on 2021-03-24.
landing.model.btcDvol.tooltip: Deribit's options-implied volatility index (DVOL) — a forward-looking counterpart to Realized Volatility, with history since 2021 only.
